text: Code refactoring is the process of improving your code's structure without changing what it actually does. Think of it like cleaning and reorganizing a messy room: everything is still there, but it's much easier to find things. By breaking large functions into smaller, clearer parts and giving variables better names, you make your code more readable for yourself and others. This reduces technical debt—the hidden cost of messy code that slows down future development. Regular refactoring keeps your codebase healthy and maintainable, saving time and frustration in the long run.
